title: CIL Geology — Full-length Mock 08
pattern: CIL MT — 200 MCQ · 2 papers · 1 mark each · no negative marking

questions.solution: USA, Canada, Mexico

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A married B. Their daughter C married D. E is the son of C. How is B related to E?
questions.solution: Grandmother

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.solution: N

questions.stem: If CHAIR = 39, TABLE = 48, DESK = 33, then BENCH = ?

questions.stem: In a 3×3 grid of squares, how many rectangles?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Should CIL diversify into renewable energy? Arg I: Yes, it will future-proof the company. Arg II: No, it should focus on its core coal business.
questions.solution: Both are strong

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Pie chart: education 25%, health 20%, defence 15%, infrastructure 30%, others 10%. Total budget ₹2,00,000 cr. Defence spending:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Pointing to a man, a woman says, 'His brother is the father of my only son'. How is woman related to the man?
questions.solution: Sister

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Book : Author :: Law : ?
questions.solution: Legislature

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If MASTER = 444, STUDENT = 606, LEARN = ?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: How many straight lines can be drawn through 10 points no three collinear?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Should mine safety inspections be conducted without prior notice? Arg I: Yes, it catches real compliance status. Arg II: No, it may disrupt operations.
questions.solution: Only I is strong

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A company's revenue grows 10% each year. If revenue in 2024 was ₹100 cr, the 2026 revenue is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A is the father of B and C. B is the mother of D. E is the daughter of D. How is A related to E?
questions.solution: Great-grandfather

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Water : Thirst :: Food : ?
questions.solution: Hunger

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In code, 'coal mining' = pk st, 'deep mining' = st mz, 'coal reserve' = pk nq. Code for 'reserve':
questions.solution: nq

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: How many triangles in a regular hexagon with all diagonals drawn?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Should the MMDR Act be further amended to allow private mining? Arg I: Yes, it will increase competition. Arg II: No, public sector is more accountable.
questions.solution: Both are strong

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Among 6 friends, P is taller than Q but shorter than R. S is taller than T but shorter than Q. U is shortest. Third tallest:
questions.solution: S

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Should mine closure plans be approved before mining begins? Arg I: Yes, ensures land reclamation commitment. Arg II: No, conditions may change over decades.
questions.solution: Both are strong

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the missing: B, C, E, H, L, ?
questions.solution: Q

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the wrong term: 2, 5, 10, 17, 28, 37, 50

questions.stem: 5 people A,B,C,D,E sit in a row. C sits in middle. A and B must be at ends. D is left of C. E sits:
questions.solution: 2nd from right

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Five people sit around a table. A faces B. C is left of A. D is between B and C. E is right of A. Who faces C?
questions.solution: D

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next: 2, 5, 10, 17, 26, ?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statements: All birds have wings. All animals with wings can fly. Some animals that fly are not birds. Conclusions: I. All birds can fly. II. Some flying animals are not birds.
questions.solution: Both follow

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The organisation ____ its doors to the public.
questions.solution: opened

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The number of accidents have increased.
options: A number of accidents has increased.
The number of accidents has increased.
The number of accidents is increasing.
The number of accidents have increased.
questions.solution: The number of accidents has increased.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: She is ____ of the fact that she made a mistake.
questions.solution: unaware

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: He is one of those who has excelled in the field.
options: He is one of those who have excelled in the field.
He is one who has excelled in the field.
He is one of those who excelled in the field.
He is one of those who has excelled in the field.
questions.solution: He is one of those who have excelled in the field.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The evidence ____ against the accused.
questions.solution: testified

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The police has arrested the suspect last night.
options: The police have arrested the suspect last night.
The police arrested the suspect last night.
The police has arrested the suspect last night.
The police had arrested the suspect last night.
questions.solution: The police arrested the suspect last night.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The new regulation ____ into effect next month.
questions.solution: comes

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The committee were divided in their opinions.
options: The committee has divided in their opinions.
The committee was divided in their opinions.
The committee is divided in its opinions.
The committee were divided in their opinions.
questions.solution: The committee were divided in their opinions.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: She is well ____ in the field of paleontology.
questions.solution: versed

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: It is high time we leave for the station.
options: It is high time we leave for the station.
It is high time we have left for the station.
It is high time we were leaving for the station.
It is high time we left for the station.
questions.solution: It is high time we left for the station.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The organisation is ____ to the cause of education.
questions.solution: committed

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: He has been working here since five years.
options: He has been working here for five years.
He has worked here since five years.
He worked here since five years.
He is working here since five years.
questions.solution: He has been working here for five years.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The report ____ several recommendations.
questions.solution: contains

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: She not only lost her wallet but also her phone.
options: She not only lost her wallet but also lost.
She lost not only her wallet but also.
She not only lost her wallet but also her phone.
She lost not only her wallet but also her phone.
questions.solution: She lost not only her wallet but also her phone.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The new technology is ____ adopted across the sector.
questions.solution: widely

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: He is more smarter than his brother.
options: He is more smarter than his brother.
He is smart than his brother.
He is smartest than his brother.
He is smarter than his brother.
questions.solution: He is smarter than his brother.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The document ____ the terms of the agreement.
questions.solution: specifies

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of SURREPTITIOUS:
questions.solution: Secret

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The road is lined ____ trees on both sides.
questions.solution: with

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The company has ____ its production targets.
questions.solution: achieved

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of INNOCUOUS:
questions.solution: Harmful

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To break the ice':
questions.solution: To initiate conversation

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of ELOQUENT:
questions.solution: Inarticulate

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'The ball is in your court':
options: It is your turn to act
questions.solution: It is your turn to act

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of EPHEMERAL:
questions.solution: Eternal

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Basalt composition:
questions.solution: Basalt: fine-grained, mafic. Most abundant volcanic rock. Oceanic crust.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Reaction rims (corona texture):
options: Incomplete reaction — mineral replacement textures. Olivine rimmed by orthopyroxene + spinel (granulite)
questions.solution: Corona: cooling (Opx + Sp around Ol). Kelyphite (Cpx + Sp around Grt). Reaction progress.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Coastal — wave-cut platform:
options: Flat, gently sloping surface cut by wave erosion at cliff base
questions.solution: Platform: erosional. Marine terrace: uplifted platform. Abrasion by sand and pebbles.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Normal mid-ocean ridge basalt (N-MORB):
options: Depleted mantle source (light REE depleted). (La/Sm)_N < 1. ~6-8% partial melting of spinel lherzolite
questions.solution: N-MORB: La/Sm < 1. Depleted in incompatible elements. E-MORB: slightly enriched.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Water table fluctuation method:
questions.solution: WTF: water table rise from precipitation. S_y from specific yield tests.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Crystal symmetry elements: rotation axes, mirror planes, inversion centers:
options: Combination defines point group (32 crystallographic point groups)
questions.solution: Point group: symmetry operations. Space group: point group + translations = 230 groups.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A dipping coal seam outcrops over a horizontal width of 80 m on flat ground and dips at 20°. Its true thickness is about:
questions.solution: True thickness = horizontal width × sin(dip) = 80×sin20° ≈ 27.36 m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: From the surface inward, the major layers of the Earth are:
questions.solution: The Earth is layered into a thin crust, a thick mantle, and a metallic core.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Twinning in plagioclase feldspar:
questions.solution: Plagioclase: polysynthetic twinning. Causes striations.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: C-14 dating:
options: ¹⁴C (T½ = 5730 yr). For organic material <50 ka. Atmospheric production, calibration curve
questions.solution: C-14: radiocarbon. β⁻ decay. Libby (1949). Calibration: tree rings, corals, speleothems.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Geotechnical investigation borehole spacing depends on:
questions.solution: Borehole spacing: simple terrain large structures ~30-50m. Complex terrain small structures ~5-15m. BS 5930 guidance.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Seismic P-wave velocity increases with:
options: Increasing rock density and elastic modulus
questions.solution: P-wave: Vp = sqrt((K+4/3G)/ρ). Higher density and modulus = higher velocity. Porosity decreases velocity.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Cross-bedding dip direction indicates:
questions.solution: Cross-bedding: foresets dip in flow direction. Used for paleocurrent analysis. Restores paleogeography.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Diagenetic environment — meteoric phreatic:
options: Freshwater-saturated zone below water table. Equant/relict calcite cement. Dissolution common
questions.solution: Meteoric phreatic: stable isotopes (δ¹⁸O depleted). Vadose: meniscus, gravitational cement.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Lineation in metamorphic rocks indicates:
questions.solution: Stretching lineation: alignment of elongate minerals. Indicates transport/stretching direction. Key kinematic indicator.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Dyke:
questions.solution: Dyke: vertical or steeply dipping. Radiating or linear swarms.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ultrahigh Temperature (UHT) metamorphism:
options: >900°C at 7-13 kbar. Sapphirine, orthopyroxene + sillimanite, osumilite. Lower crust
questions.solution: UHT: peak T >900°C. Mg-Al granulites. Eastern Ghats, Napier Complex (Antarctica).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Object-based image analysis (OBIA):
options: Segment image → classify segments (not pixels). Shape, texture, context. eCognition
questions.solution: OBIA: multiresolution segmentation. Scale parameter. Feature space. Better than pixel for high-res.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sequence boundary (SB):
options: Subaerial unconformity + correlative conformity. Type 1 (Shelf exposed) vs Type 2 (shelf not exposed)
questions.solution: SB: base-level fall. Vail et al. (1977). Exxon sequence stratigraphy. Lowstand fan.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Crystal system of topaz:
questions.solution: Topaz (Al2SiO4(F,OH)2) — orthorhombic. Hardness 8.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Gneiss is characterised by:
options: Banding (segregation of light and dark minerals)
questions.solution: Gneiss is a high-grade foliated rock showing compositional banding (gneissic foliation).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal seam covers 1 km², is 3 m thick, with density 1.3 t/m³. The in-situ reserve is about:
questions.solution: Reserve = area×thickness×density = 1×10⁶ m²×3 m×1.3 t/m³ ≈ 3.9 Mt.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Accretionary wedge: deformed sediments scraped off subducting plate:
questions.solution: Accretion: prism of deformed sediment. Offscraping, underplating. Mélanges: blocks in matrix.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Debrites (debris flow deposits):
options: Matrix-supported, poorly sorted, angular clasts, no grading
questions.solution: Debris flow: cohesive matrix support. High viscosity. Liquefied flow: fluid support.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Extinction angle in minerals:
options: Angle between cleavage and vibration direction under crossed polars
questions.solution: Straight extinction: 0°. Oblique: 0-45°. Symmetrical: angle to both cleavages.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Metamorphic rocks form by the action of:
options: Heat and/or pressure on pre-existing rocks
questions.solution: Metamorphism alters mineralogy and texture in the solid state under heat/pressure.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Nannofossils (coccoliths):
options: Calcareous nannoplankton (<30 µm). Cretaceous-Recent. Biostratigraphy + paleoclimate. Emiliania huxleyi
questions.solution: Nannofossils: produced by haptophyte algae. Chalk. High-resolution zonation for Cretaceous-Tertiary.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Factors of metamorphism:
questions.solution: T drives reactions. P controls mineral density. Fluids catalyze reactions.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Tsunamis:
options: Ocean wave from underwater earthquake (M>7, thrust fault). Velocity ~√(gh)
questions.solution: Tsunami: long wavelength (>100 km). Wave height increases in shallow water. Run-up.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An aquifer (K = 10 m/day) of cross-section 100 m² has a hydraulic gradient of 0.01. The groundwater discharge is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Unit cell: smallest repeating unit. For cubic: a=b=c, α=β=γ=90°. Volume:
questions.solution: Cubic: V=a³. Tetragonal: V=a²c. Orthorhombic: V=abc. Hexagonal: V=a²c·sin(60°).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Trilobite eyes: compound eyes made of:
questions.solution: Trilobite eyes: calcite lenses. Holochroal (many lenses) or schizochroal (fewer, larger lenses).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cosmogenic nuclide exposure dating (¹⁰Be, ²⁶Al):
options: Nucleides produced by cosmic rays in surface rocks. Higher concentration = longer exposure
questions.solution: ¹⁰Be in quartz. Exposure age = N/(P×(1-e^{-λt})). For surfaces >100ka.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Kriging interpolation:
options: Best linear unbiased estimator (BLUE). Uses variogram. Weighted average of neighbors
questions.solution: Kriging: minimize estimation variance. Weights from variogram. Ordinary: unknown mean. Simple: known mean.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An ore body of 1 Mt has an average metal grade of 3%. The contained metal is about:
questions.solution: Metal = tonnage × grade = 1 Mt × 3% = 30 thousand tonnes (kt).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Epithermal gold deposit:
options: Low sulfidation (quartz-adularia) vs high sulfidation (enargite). Hot springs. Au-Ag
questions.solution: Epithermal: <300°C, <1.5 km. Hishikari (Japan), Yanacocha (Peru). Bonanza grades.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Coal bed methane (CBM):
options: Methane adsorbed in coal matrix. Sweet spots: high rank, good permeability. Recovery: dewatering → desorption
questions.solution: CBM: shallow (300-1500m). Proppantless fracturing. Coal rank: high-volatile bituminous best.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Volcanogenic Massive Sulfide (VMS):
options: Cu-Zn-Pb ± Au-Ag. Submarine volcanic exhalative. Kuroko (Japan), Noranda, Kidd Creek
questions.solution: VMS: stratiform, lens-shaped. Stringer zone (feeder) + massive ore. Besshi, Cyprus, Kuroko types.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rock retains 25% of its original parent isotope (half-life 1.3 Gyr). Its radiometric age is about:
questions.solution: 25% remaining ⇒ 2 half-lives ⇒ age = 2×1.3 = 2.6 Gyr.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the block diagram shown, the inclined bedding plane dips at 30°. The dip direction is, by definition:
options: Perpendicular to the strike, in the direction of steepest descent
questions.solution: Dip (here 30°) is measured perpendicular to strike, down the line of steepest slope of the plane.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 240 130" width="240" height="130"><polygon points="20,40 200,40 220,70 40,70" fill="#e2e8f0" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><line x1="40" y1="70" x2="220" y2="70" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><text x="120" y="86" font-size="9" fill="#2563eb">strike</text><line x1="120" y1="70" x2="160" y2="110" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><text x="150" y="100" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">dip 30°</text><path d="M120 70 A 30 30 0 0 1 150 92" fill="none" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1"/></svg>
figure.caption: Strike and dip of a bed

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal sample (proximate analysis) has 4% moisture, 25% volatile matter and 15% ash. Its fixed carbon is:
questions.solution: Fixed C = 100 − (moisture + VM + ash) = 100 − (4+25+15) = 56%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Darcy's law:
options: Q = K·i·A. K = hydraulic conductivity, i = hydraulic gradient, A = area
questions.solution: Darcy: laminar flow. Valid for Re < 10. K = k·ρ·g/μ (intrinsic permeability).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The most abundant group of rock-forming minerals in the Earth's crust is the:
questions.solution: Silicate minerals (feldspar, quartz, mica, etc.) dominate the crust.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hypsometric curve:
options: Erosion stage (youth, mature, old). Area-altitude relationship. Young = convex (upper), old = concave (lower)
questions.solution: Hypsometry: integral = area under curve. High integral > 0.6 (youthful).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Supervised classification:
options: Training samples (known classes) → classify rest of image. Maximum likelihood
questions.solution: Supervised: user-defined training areas. Unsupervised: algorithm clusters pixels. Hybrid approach common.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Earthquake magnitude vs intensity:
questions.solution: Moment magnitude (Mw): most accurate. MMI: I (not felt) to XII (total destruction).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Bowen's reaction series: discontinuous branch (mafic minerals):
options: Olivine -> Pyroxene -> Amphibole -> Biotite (increasing silica content)
questions.solution: Discontinuous: olivine (early, high T) to biotite (late, lower T). Continuous: Ca-plagioclase to Na-plagioclase.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Siwalik Group divisions:
options: Lower (Kamliyal), Middle (Nagri, Dhok Pathan), Upper (Tatrot, Pinjor, Boulder Conglomerate)
questions.solution: Siwalik: molasse (Himalayan erosion). Hominoid fossils (Ramapithecus). Thickness >5000m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Carbonate diagenesis — neomorphism:
options: Replacement of aragonite or calcite by calcite of different crystal size (aggrading neomorphism)
questions.solution: Neomorphism: wet transformation. Microspar → pseudospar. Aragonite → calcite inversion.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Contact (thermal) metamorphism:
options: Heat from intrusion. Aureole around pluton
questions.solution: Contact: high T, low P. Hornfels. Spotted slates near intrusion.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The fastest seismic body waves, which arrive first at a station, are:
questions.solution: P-waves are compressional and travel fastest, arriving before slower S and surface waves.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Geostatistics: variogram model:
options: Semivariance vs distance. Sill = total variance. Range = correlation length
questions.solution: Variogram: γ(h) = ½E[(Z(x)-Z(x+h))²]. Spherical, exponential, Gaussian models. Nugget effect.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rock retains 50% of its original parent isotope (half-life 1.3 Gyr). Its radiometric age is about:
questions.solution: 50% remaining ⇒ 1 half-lives ⇒ age = 1×1.3 = 1.3 Gyr.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Porosity in fault zones:
options: Core: low porosity (gouge). Damage zone: high fracture porosity. Conduit-barrier
questions.solution: Fault seal: clay smear, cataclasis. Juxtaposition seal. Membrane seal (capillary entry pressure).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Coal quality parameters for coking coal:
options: Caking index (GRI), CRI (coke reactivity), CSR (coke strength after reaction)
questions.solution: Coking: GRI >70 for good coking. CRI <25, CSR >65 for blast furnace coke.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Transmissivity T = K*b. For K=10 m/day, aquifer thickness b=20m:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Apatite hardness:
questions.solution: Ca5(PO4)3(F,Cl,OH). Index mineral #5. Phosphate ore.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Glacial landforms:
options: U-shaped valley, cirque, arête, horn, hanging valley, moraine (lateral, medial, terminal)
questions.solution: Glacial: erosion (U-valley) and deposition (moraine, drumlin, esker, till).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Landslide susceptibility mapping:
options: Weighted overlay: slope, geology, rainfall, land use, distance to faults
questions.solution: Susceptibility: heuristic, statistical (logistic regression), deterministic (infinite slope). Machine learning.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Squeezing ground in tunnels:
options: High stress in weak rock → plastic deformation. Invert struts needed
questions.solution: Squeezing: ductile deformation. Caused by high overburden, weak rock (shale).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Lamprophyre: volatile-rich, porphyritic mafic igneous rock. Phenocrysts typically:
options: Amphibole, biotite, or olivine in fine groundmass
questions.solution: Lamprophyre: lamprophyric texture. Dark minerals as phenocrysts. Often associated with diamond pipes.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Principle of original horizontality:
questions.solution: Steno: tilted beds indicate post-depositional deformation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hornblende hornfels facies:
options: Contact metamorphism, moderate grade. Plagioclase + hornblende + cordierite
questions.solution: Hornfels facies: pyroxene-hornfels (high), hornblende-hornfels (medium), albite-epidote (low).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An ore body of 5 Mt has an average metal grade of 1%. The contained metal is about:
questions.solution: Metal = tonnage × grade = 5 Mt × 1% = 50 thousand tonnes (kt).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal seam covers 5 km², is 3 m thick, with density 1.3 t/m³. The in-situ reserve is about:
questions.solution: Reserve = area×thickness×density = 5×10⁶ m²×3 m×1.3 t/m³ ≈ 19.5 Mt.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A mineral specimen of mass 78 g has a volume of 20 cm³. Its specific gravity is about:
questions.solution: SG = mass/volume = 78/20 = 3.9.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Debris flow mitigation:
options: Check dams, deflection walls, sabo dams, early warning
questions.solution: Debris flow: high velocity mixture of soil, rock, water. Runout distance analysis.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mudstone:
options: Lithified mud (silt + clay). Massive, no fissility
questions.solution: Mudstone vs shale: shale splits into thin layers (fissile).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Using a simplified proxy where calorific value ≈ 70 × fixed-carbon%, a coal with 40% fixed carbon has a calorific value of about:
questions.solution: CV ≈ 70 × 40 = 2800 kcal/kg (illustrative proxy).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Classification accuracy assessment:
options: Confusion matrix. Overall accuracy, producer's accuracy, user's accuracy. Kappa coefficient
questions.solution: Confusion matrix: errors of omission (producer's) and commission (user's). Kappa: chance-corrected agreement.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A bed dipping at 30° shows a width of 30 m across a horizontal surface. Its true (perpendicular) thickness is about:
questions.solution: True thickness = width × sin(dip) = 30×sin30° ≈ 15 m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: GPS in geology:
options: Crustal deformation monitoring (mm precision). Plate motion ~cm/yr
questions.solution: GPS: continuous GPS stations. Earthquake cycle: interseismic, coseismic, postseismic deformation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fault gouge thickness typically:
options: Increases with displacement and decreases with depth
questions.solution: Gouge thickness: t ∝ d^α (α≈0.5). Thicker at shallow depth (more brittle).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Rhyolite vs granite: same composition but:
options: Rhyolite: volcanic (fine-grained or glassy). Granite: plutonic (coarse-grained)
questions.solution: Rhyolite: volcanic equivalent of granite. Same bulk composition. Texture differs due to cooling rate.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The fold shown in cross-section (beds arch upward about the axial plane) is a(n):
questions.solution: Upward-arching limbs dipping away from the axis define an anticline (oldest beds in core).

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 130" width="260" height="130"><path d="M20 90 Q 130 20 240 90" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><path d="M20 110 Q 130 45 240 110" fill="none" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="130" y1="15" x2="130" y2="120" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="130" y="128" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">axial plane</text></svg>
figure.caption: anticline (cross-section)

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Slug test analysis (Hvorslev):
options: K = r²·ln(L/R)/(2·L·T₀). T₀ = time for 37% recovery
questions.solution: Hvorslev: for low-K formations. T₀ from semi-log plot of water level vs time.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal sample (proximate analysis) has 5% moisture, 30% volatile matter and 20% ash. Its fixed carbon is:
questions.solution: Fixed C = 100 − (moisture + VM + ash) = 100 − (5+30+20) = 45%.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Hydraulic conductivity of gravel vs clay:
questions.solution: Gravel: K = 10^2 to 10^4 m/day. Clay: 10^-9 to 10^-5.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: To extract 5 m³ of coal, 60 m³ of overburden must be removed. The stripping ratio is:
questions.solution: Stripping ratio = overburden/coal = 60/5 = 12 : 1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A bed drops 10 m over a horizontal distance of 10 m. Its dip angle is about:
questions.solution: dip = tan⁻¹(rise/run) = tan⁻¹(10/10) ≈ 45°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Crystal system of gypsum:
questions.solution: Gypsum (CaSO4·2H2O) — monoclinic. Hardness 2. Used in plaster.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Perched water table:
options: Local saturated zone above region water table due to low-permeability lens
questions.solution: Perched: clay lens creates local saturation. Limited yield. Shallow wells.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal sample (proximate analysis) has 8% moisture, 35% volatile matter and 15% ash. Its fixed carbon is:
questions.solution: Fixed C = 100 − (moisture + VM + ash) = 100 − (8+35+15) = 42%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Geothermobarometry — GASP:
options: Grossular + Al₂SiO₅ + Quartz → Anorthite. Pressure-sensitive. Used with Grt-Bt thermometer
questions.solution: GASP: Ca transfer. K = a_An³/(a_Grs·a_Ky²·a_Qz). P from equilibrium constant.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Balanced cross-section requires:
options: Conservation of bed length and area during restoration
questions.solution: Balanced: no gaps or overlaps after restoration. Bed length and area preserved.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Basalt is a(n):
questions.solution: Basalt is the fine-grained, dark, mafic rock formed from rapidly cooled lava.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A bed dipping at 30° shows a width of 40 m across a horizontal surface. Its true (perpendicular) thickness is about:
questions.solution: True thickness = width × sin(dip) = 40×sin30° ≈ 20 m.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Boudinage structure indicates:
questions.solution: Boudinage: layer stretched and broken into sausage-shaped blocks. Indicates extension along layering. Common in metamorphic rocks.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Wave-cut platform width controlled by:
options: Rate of sea-level change, rock resistance, wave energy
questions.solution: Platform: wider in weak rock, high wave energy, stable sea level. Narrow: resistant rock, rapid sea-level change.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal sample (proximate analysis) has 5% moisture, 35% volatile matter and 10% ash. Its fixed carbon is:
questions.solution: Fixed C = 100 − (moisture + VM + ash) = 100 − (5+35+10) = 50%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Vitrinite reflectance Ro% rank indicator: Ro < 0.5% is:
questions.solution: Lignite: Ro<0.5%. Sub-bit: 0.5-0.6%. Bituminous: 0.6-2.0%. Anthracite: >2.0%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Provenance in sedimentary geology:
options: Source area of sediments (composition, age, tectonic setting)
questions.solution: Provenance: heavy mineral analysis, zircon U-Pb dating, paleocurrents.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ar-Ar dating advantage over K-Ar:
options: Single heating step or step heating for age spectrum. No need for separate K measurement
questions.solution: Ar-Ar: irradiation converts ³⁹K to ³⁹Ar. Step heating: age spectrum. Plateau age = undisturbed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A P-wave travels at 8 km/s and arrives 20 s after the earthquake. The (approx.) distance to the source is:
questions.solution: distance = velocity × time = 8×20 = 160 km.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: To extract 15 m³ of coal, 45 m³ of overburden must be removed. The stripping ratio is:
questions.solution: Stripping ratio = overburden/coal = 45/15 = 3 : 1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Borehole geophysics:
options: Gamma, resistivity, SP, caliper, sonic logging. In-situ rock properties
questions.solution: Well logging: lithology, porosity, density, water quality. Acoustic televiewer: fractures.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Geophysical method — GPR:
options: Ground penetrating radar. High resolution, shallow (<10m)
questions.solution: GPR: electromagnetic waves. Utility detection, void mapping, archaeology.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Seismic refraction — critical refraction:
options: Head wave at critical angle (sin i_c = V₁/V₂). Refractor velocity V₂ from intercept time
questions.solution: Refraction: first arrivals. Crossover distance. Layer thickness: h = (x_c/2)√((V₂-V₁)/(V₂+V₁)).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Groundwater in hard rocks:
options: Fracture-controlled. Weathered zone (shallow) + fractured zone (deeper). Yield variable
questions.solution: Hard rock: only secondary porosity. Fracture zones, lineaments. High-yield wells along major fractures.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Earth's magnetic field: geodynamo:
options: Convection in liquid outer core. Dynamo action generates dipole field
questions.solution: Geodynamo: convection of liquid iron in outer core. Self-sustaining. Reverses periodically.
